architecture.summary: SMIXAE extends sparse autoencoders (SAEs) by introducing a mixture-of-autoencoders architecture to directly model multidimensional features in transformer activations. Instead of tiling multidimensional features across independent SAE directions (which must be grouped post-hoc), SMIXAE trains multiple SAE components whose outputs are combined, enabling unsupervised discovery of manifold structures in language model hidden states. Information flows from frozen model activations into the trained mixture components, which jointly reconstruct activation patterns.
trained_components: sparse mixture-of-autoencoders (multiple SAE components and mixture weights)

biblio.title: SMIXAE: Towards Unsupervised Manifold Discovery in Language Models

claims.statement: SAEs have an inability to directly model multidimensional features, instead tiling such features by a set of independent directions that must be grouped together after the SAE training phase, impeding discoverability and interpretation of learned feature representations
SMIXAE models have success in directly learning previously identified manifold structures within the open source Gemma 2 2B and 9B models
SMIXAE models find novel structures within the open source Gemma 2 2B and 9B models
